name: File Gumbo
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 7-quart stock pot, bring the chicken to a boil until the meat separates from the bone.
2. Remove the cooked meat and return the bones and all organs (except liver) to simmer for half an hour to make stock. Strain and skim off any fat. Return the strained stock to the pot.
3. In a cast iron skillet, heat butter and add flour. Stir with a wooden spoon until the roux reaches a dark brown color.
4. Add onions, celery, scallions, and bell pepper to the skillet. Sauté for five minutes.
5. Pour the sautéed vegetables into the stock pot.
6. In the same skillet, lightly brown the chicken and Andouille sausage with garlic, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper.
7. Drain any excess fat from the sausage, then add it to the stock along with one bay leaf.
8. Simmer for one hour at a very low boil.
9. Just before serving, sprinkle file powder over the gumbo and serve alongside a bowl of rice.
